title: Verify button primitive accessibility
description:
## Summary
Verify button/link/toolbar primitives before migrating all toolbar actions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every icon-only control pattern has an accessible name.
- Focus rings are visible against toolbar and pane backgrounds.
- Keyboard activation works for buttons and links without stealing graph/editor focus unexpectedly.

## Design Notes
- Brutalist/minimal styling should make focus and disabled states more explicit than Material defaults.

## Edge Cases
- Disabled controls, external link focus, menu trigger focus restoration, and high contrast mode.

## Testing Strategy
- Manual keyboard pass plus targeted Cypress assertions where current helpers can verify labels/disabled states.

title: Build button and link control variants
description:
## Summary
Build app-owned button/link primitives for icon-only, icon+text, text, disabled, active, and external-link controls.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Primitives preserve stable IDs, aria labels, click handler pass-through, disabled behavior, and focus states.
- External links preserve href/target/rel behavior and accessible names.
- Styling uses tokens, rectangular geometry, and no Material classes/shadows.

## Design Notes
- Controls should feel precise and compact, not decorative.

## Edge Cases
- Icon-only accessible names, disabled undo/redo, GitHub external link, and high browser zoom.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through toolbar migration and github/undo_redo specs.

title: Build toolbar grouping and layout primitives
description:
## Summary
Build primitives for toolbar root, command groups, separators, title/name area, and responsive wrapping/overflow behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Supports current toolbar command grouping without layout shifts on disabled/active state changes.
- Provides stable dimensions and responsive constraints for standard and narrow viewports.
- Long graph names are contained without hiding core controls.

## Design Notes
- Use visible command grouping and compact grid/flex rules instead of Material Toolbar/AppBar behavior.

## Edge Cases
- Narrow viewport, long graph name, browser zoom, and fullscreen entry/exit.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through ButtonAppBar migration and responsive toolbar checks.

title: Capture overlay storage and settings baselines
description:
## Summary
Capture current behavior for dialogs, drawers, menus, storage, export, help, and settings workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Baseline covers main menu, help menu, settings, open from browser, save/rename, replace/delete confirmations, export URL, export SVG, format drawers, insert panels, keyboard shortcuts, mouse operations, and about dialog.
- Existing Cypress specs covering these workflows are listed with pass/fail state.
- Dialog focus/escape behavior and stable IDs are noted where relevant.

## Design Notes
- Record workflow behavior and selector expectations, not Material visual details.

## Edge Cases
- Nested confirmations, empty saved graph list, duplicate names, long DOT snippets, and color picker visibility.

## Testing Strategy
- Run representative main_menu, browser_save_and_open, export/import, settings, help, and insert/format related checks.

title: Capture responsive backend and notification baselines
description:
## Summary
Capture current behavior for app-level states that are easy to miss during visual migration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Baseline covers narrow viewport layout, long graph names, update snackbar visibility/close behavior, localStorage restoration, URL import, backend SSE reconnect expectations, and graph state synchronization assumptions.
- Any behavior without automated coverage is marked as manual verification with reproduction steps.
- Baseline notes identify which states should be included in visual smoke coverage.

## Design Notes
- These states often fail because of layout, portal, or global CSS changes; record them before touching the shell.

## Edge Cases
- First load after version change, browser reload with persisted project, backend unavailable, and narrow viewport dialogs.

## Testing Strategy
- Run smoke/manual checks alongside any existing Cypress specs that touch these states.

title: Capture graph and editor workflow baselines
description:
## Summary
Capture current behavior for core graph and DOT editor workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Baseline covers initial render, DOT edit/render loop, parser errors, text editor font/tab/holdoff behavior, graph pan/zoom, fullscreen, visual insert, draw edge, select/deselect, delete, undo, and redo.
- Existing Cypress specs covering these workflows are listed with pass/fail state.
- Manual-only observations include concrete reproduction steps.

## Design Notes
- Do not propose visual changes here; record behavior only.

## Edge Cases
- Malformed DOT, slow render holdoff, transition animation, selected edge markers, and fullscreen exit.

## Testing Strategy
- Run representative rendering, text_editor, pan_and_zoom, fullscreen_graph, insert_node, draw_edge, select/delete, transition, and undo_redo coverage.

title: Verify graph chrome regression coverage
description:
## Summary
Run targeted Graph.js regression checks after graph chrome migration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rendering, fullscreen, pan/zoom, selection/delete, draw edge, and transition specs pass or failures are filed as P0 regressions.
- No CSS reset or graph chrome style breaks generated SVG labels, paths, polygons, or markers.
- Results are recorded in the graph/editor verification task.

## Design Notes
- This is the local gate before the broader graph/editor regression pass.

## Edge Cases
- Selected edges with markers, large graphs, malformed DOT recovery, and fullscreen exit.

## Testing Strategy
- Run the targeted Cypress specs named in the acceptance criteria.

title: Build select switch checkbox and radio primitives
description:
## Summary
Build option-control primitives for replacing MUI Select, Switch, Checkbox, and RadioGroup usage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Select supports current engine selection behavior and stable option IDs.
- Switch and checkbox controls preserve event.target.checked semantics.
- Radio groups expose accessible group labels and preserve current value changes.

## Design Notes
- Binary and option states should be visibly explicit without Material color conventions.

## Edge Cases
- Generated engine list, hidden/disabled style checkboxes, tween precision unit changes, and keyboard selection.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through SettingsDialog and FormatDrawer migrations.

title: Verify form event and accessibility contracts
description:
## Summary
Verify primitive event contracts before broad form migration begins.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Text/number/select controls produce the same event shapes current handlers expect.
- Switch/checkbox/radio controls produce the same checked/value semantics current handlers expect.
- Labels, helper text, invalid states, disabled states, and keyboard operation are documented and tested through first adopters.

## Design Notes
- This is a behavior safety gate for form migration.

## Edge Cases
- LocalStorage string values, adornment text changes, hidden controls, and rapid color/input changes.

## Testing Strategy
- Run targeted settings and format drawer checks after first primitive adoption.

title: Verify portal layering and focus behavior
description:
## Summary
Verify overlay primitives before broad dialog/menu/drawer migration begins.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dialog, menu, tooltip, drawer, and snackbar examples layer above graph/editor surfaces and below/above each other intentionally.
- Escape, outside click, and focus restoration behavior is documented for each primitive family.
- Reduced-motion and body scroll locking policies are documented.

## Design Notes
- This is the foundation-level overlay safety gate.

## Edge Cases
- Graph fullscreen plus dialog, nested alert dialog, menu opened from toolbar, and editor focused before tooltip/dialog.

## Testing Strategy
- Run a targeted manual pass and any available Cypress smoke checks after first primitive adoption.

title: Build text and number field primitives
description:
## Summary
Build input primitives for text and numeric settings/storage fields.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Supports label, helper text, adornment, value, change, blur, disabled, invalid, and stable IDs.
- Preserves event.target.value semantics expected by existing handlers.
- Numeric inputs do not coerce string-backed localStorage values unless existing code already does.

## Design Notes
- Fields should be compact, high-contrast, and easy to scan in dense dialogs/drawers.

## Edge Cases
- Empty values, long names, decimal values, percent/points adornments, and browser autofill.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through SettingsDialog, SaveAsToBrowserDialog, ExportAsSvgDialog, ExportAsUrlDialog, and ColorPicker migrations.

title: Build drawer primitive
description:
## Summary
Build the app-owned drawer primitive used by node and edge format inspectors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Supports fixed side placement, stable root/content IDs, close control, focus behavior, and layout integration with the graph/editor panes.
- Drawer styling uses tokens for width, border, background, and section rhythm.
- Closing the drawer calls existing handlers without changing graph selection state.

## Design Notes
- Drawers should behave like inspector panels, not modal cards.

## Edge Cases
- Left/right placement, fullscreen graph mode, hidden color pickers, and narrow viewport widths.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through FormatDrawer migration.

title: Build dialog and alert-dialog primitives
description:
## Summary
Build the app-owned dialog/alert-dialog wrappers used by settings, storage/export, help/about, and confirmation flows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Supports title, description, content, close button, action row, backdrop, escape, outside interaction policy, focus trap, and focus restoration.
- Stable IDs can be placed on dialog roots and action buttons without relying on generated Base UI structure.
- Confirmation dialogs can visually distinguish destructive actions without changing callback contracts.

## Design Notes
- Use sharp frames, compact header/action rows, and no Material shadows.

## Edge Cases
- Nested confirmation inside open-from-browser/save flows, escape handling, and graph fullscreen layering.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate first with SettingsDialog and destructive confirmation migration.

title: Build menu popover and tooltip primitives
description:
## Summary
Build app-owned primitives for command menus, small popovers, and tooltips used by toolbar, main menu, help menu, table actions, and editor error affordances.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Supports anchored positioning, keyboard navigation where applicable, escape/outside close, accessible labels, and stable trigger/content IDs.
- Tooltip behavior does not steal focus from editor or graph interactions.
- Menus can preserve current command IDs and click handlers.

## Design Notes
- Menus should read as compact command lists with high-contrast row states.

## Edge Cases
- Toolbar menus near viewport edges, narrow widths, and portaled content over graph SVG.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through MainMenu, HelpMenu, OpenFromBrowserDialog delete tooltip, and TextEditor error tooltip migrations.

title: Build disclosure and collapsible primitives
description:
## Summary
Create app-owned disclosure/collapsible primitives for replacing MUI Accordion usage in insert panels and any future compact sections.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Primitive supports controlled and uncontrolled open state, stable IDs on triggers/content, keyboard activation, and accessible expanded/collapsed state.
- Styling uses brutalist/minimal tokens: sharp separators, compact trigger rows, and clear open-state affordance.
- InsertPanels can use the primitive without changing shape insertion, click, or drag behavior.

## Design Notes
- This belongs outside overlay primitives; accordions/collapsibles are in-flow disclosure, not popups.

## Edge Cases
- Long shape category names, many categories open, narrow insert panel width, and drag start from inside a disclosed section.

## Testing Strategy
- Verify with InsertPanels migration and insert_node Cypress coverage.

title: Select custom font pair and licensing strategy
description:
## Summary
Choose the custom fonts and loading approach before global typography is implemented.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Select a display/control font and a body/UI font that fit a brutalist/minimal graph editor and are not Roboto, Arial, Inter, Open Sans, or default system fonts as the primary identity.
- Confirm license, package/source, fallback stack, font-display behavior, and whether fonts are bundled locally or installed as dependencies.
- Document how Ace editor font-size settings remain independent from app chrome typography.

## Design Notes
- Typography should make the app feel like a precise editing instrument: compact, legible, distinctive, and non-Material.

## Edge Cases
- Offline/local development, build output size, browser font fallback, and Graphviz-generated SVG labels.

## Testing Strategy
- Verify chosen fonts render in app chrome without changing Ace editor content sizing or Graphviz SVG output.

title: Map protected workflows to regression coverage
description:
## Summary
Map every protected user workflow to current automated coverage, new smoke coverage, or an explicit manual verification step before UI migration begins.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The map covers toolbar actions, main/help menus, DOT edit/render, graph pan/zoom, fullscreen, visual insert/draw/select/delete, undo/redo, settings, storage, export/import, help/about, snackbar, backend sync, and localStorage restoration.
- Each workflow references an existing Cypress/Jest spec, a planned smoke assertion, or a named manual check with reproduction steps.
- Uncovered high-risk workflows are turned into follow-up Beads before component migration starts.

## Design Notes
- This is a coverage map, not a redesign artifact. It exists to keep implementation slices honest.

## Edge Cases
- Include workflows that are easy to miss because they are passive: update snackbar, backend SSE reconnect, persisted graph name, and graph transition timing.

## Testing Strategy
- Cross-check against cypress/e2e, cypress/support/commands.js, README implemented features, and current MUI touchpoint audit.

title: Re-run regression suite after QA fixes
description:
Summary:
Run the final automated regression pass after shell/overlay and graph/editor QA fixes are complete.

Acceptance criteria:
- Jest, production build, and Cypress regression coverage pass or have documented environment limitations with equivalent targeted coverage.
- No P0 redesign regression remains open.
- Results are recorded for release handoff.

Design notes:
- This is the release gate for retiring Material UI, not the first discovery pass.

Edge cases:
- Regressions fixed in one area must not re-break another migrated surface.

Testing strategy:
- Re-run failed specs from the first pass, then run the broadest practical full suite.

title: Fix toolbar menu and dialog regressions found during QA
description:
## Summary
Address any shell, toolbar, menu, dialog, drawer, snackbar, or form regressions discovered during final QA.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Complete the shell, overlay, form, graph/editor, and dependency-cleanup migration tasks.
2. Run the first full automated regression pass in gviz-4wn.3.
3. Reproduce any failures that involve toolbar, menus, dialogs, drawers, snackbar, forms, focus, escape, selectors, or persisted setting behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every failing shell/overlay test has a fix or a clearly justified test update for equivalent behavior.
- Escape, focus restoration, click handlers, IDs, and persisted setting flows pass after fixes.
- No fix reintroduces MUI dependencies.

## Design Notes
- Keep fixes behavior-first; visual refinements can be follow-up tasks unless they block usability.

## Edge Cases
- Nested confirmations, portaled menus, narrow toolbar, and snackbar offset.

## Testing Strategy
- Rerun the failing specs plus shell/overlay smoke checks.

title: Fix graph and editor regressions found during QA
description:
## Summary
Address any graph canvas, SVG rendering, DOT editor, selection, pan/zoom, transition, or visual editing regressions discovered during final QA.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Complete the shell, overlay, form, graph/editor, and dependency-cleanup migration tasks.
2. Run the first full automated regression pass in gviz-4wn.3.
3. Reproduce any failures that involve DOT editing, graph rendering, visual editing, generated SVG, selection, pan/zoom, transitions, fullscreen, or backend sync behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every failing graph/editor test has a fix or a clearly justified test update for equivalent behavior.
- Graph parsing, mutation, selection, deletion, undo/redo, and generated SVG IDs/classes remain stable.
- No fix changes DOT semantics or backend sync contracts.

## Design Notes
- Keep graph/editor content visually primary and avoid CSS fixes that globally affect generated SVG unexpectedly.

## Edge Cases
- Large graphs, malformed DOT, fullscreen, transition animation, and selected edges with markers.

## Testing Strategy
- Rerun failing specs plus rendering, text_editor, pan_and_zoom, select/delete, draw_edge, transition, and fullscreen_graph specs.

title: Remove obsolete dependencies and lockfile entries
description:
Summary:
Remove unused direct dependencies from package.json and regenerate the lockfile after MUI migration.

Acceptance criteria:
- Direct dependencies no longer include unused @mui/material, @mui/icons-material, @mui/codemod, @emotion/react, @emotion/styled, tss-react, or typeface-roboto.
- Base UI and any chosen icon/font packages remain declared.
- Lockfile is updated through the package manager, not edited manually.

Design notes:
- Dependency removal should happen only after source imports are gone.

Edge cases:
- Some packages may remain transitive; do not fight the lockfile if another dependency needs them.

Testing strategy:
- Run npm install or npm prune as appropriate, then npm test -- --watchAll=false and npm run build.

title: Run full automated regression suite
description:
Summary:
Run the complete automated test/build suite after dependency cleanup and triage the result.

Acceptance criteria:
- Jest tests are run and pass, or failures are captured with concrete follow-up bug tasks.
- Production build is run and passes, or failures are captured with concrete follow-up bug tasks.
- Cypress specs are run, or any environment-only limitation is documented with the targeted specs that did run.
- Test output is attached to the handoff notes for the redesign branch.

Design notes:
- This is the first full regression pass after Material UI removal; failures should unblock focused bug-fix tasks rather than be hidden.

Edge cases:
- Cypress may require backend/dev server orchestration and browser availability.
- Graphviz build step can emit known warnings; distinguish warnings from failures.

Testing strategy:
- Run npm test -- --watchAll=false, npm run build, npm run backend/start:local as needed, and npm run integration-test or targeted Cypress specs.

title: Retire Material UI after regression proof
description:
## Summary
Remove Material UI, tss-react, Roboto, and obsolete styling paths only after migrated UI surfaces pass regression checks.

## Success Criteria
- package.json and lockfile no longer include unused Material UI, Emotion, tss-react, @mui/icons-material, @mui/codemod, or typeface-roboto dependencies after verification confirms they are unused.
- rg finds no @mui, tss-react/mui, withStyles, useTheme, CssBaseline, ThemeProvider, or typeface-roboto imports in application source.
- Full automated regression suite and build pass before this epic is closed.

## Design Notes
- Dependency removal is the final proof that the app owns its interface layer.

## Parallelization
- Do not start dependency removal until shell, overlay/form, and graph/editor verification tasks have passed.
- Shell/overlay QA fixes and graph/editor QA fixes can run in parallel after the first full regression pass identifies failures.
- Final release notes wait for accessibility audit and the final regression rerun.

## Edge Cases
- Emotion may still be transitive; only remove direct dependencies that are no longer imported.
- Build output and generated files should follow repo policy and avoid unrelated churn.

## Testing Strategy
- Run npm test -- --watchAll=false, npm run build, and Cypress regression suite or documented targeted subset if the full suite is impractical locally.

title: Remove residual MUI and tss imports
description:
Summary:
Search the source tree and remove any remaining direct references to Material UI, MUI icons, tss-react, and theme wrappers after component migration.

Acceptance criteria:
- rg reports no direct source imports from @mui/material, @mui/icons-material, tss-react/mui, or typeface-roboto.
- Any obsolete wrapper files are deleted or simplified.
- No functional code is changed outside UI import/styling cleanup.

Design notes:
- Cleanup should be mechanical and scoped.

Edge cases:
- GitHubIcon replacement and withRoot removal may leave dead imports or exports.

Testing strategy:
- Run lint/search checks and npm run build.

title: Verify graph and editor workflows end to end
description:
Summary:
Run the full graph/editor workflow set after work surface migration.

Acceptance criteria:
- DOT edit/render loop, insert node, draw edge, select/deselect, delete, undo/redo, pan/zoom, fullscreen, transition settings, and parser errors pass automated or documented manual checks.
- Any regression is filed or fixed before Material UI removal begins.
- No work surface component still imports MUI/tss-react after migration tasks complete.

Design notes:
- Treat any behavior drift as a blocker, even if the visual design looks complete.

Edge cases:
- Large DOT documents, malformed DOT, slow render holdoff, backend sync updates, and fullscreen interactions.

Testing strategy:
- Run targeted Cypress specs for graph/editor flows, then include them in full regression.

title: Preserve SVG graph selection and marker styling
description:
Summary:
Confirm the new global CSS and graph chrome do not break d3-graphviz generated SVG styling or selection markers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Node and edge selection markings remain visible and match current behavior.
- CSS reset does not alter generated SVG text, paths, polygons, arrowheads, or marker colors unexpectedly.
- Graph mutations still produce expected SVG IDs/classes for Cypress helpers.

Design notes:
- App CSS must be scoped carefully around generated graph SVG.

Edge cases:
- Dark fill colors, selected edges with arrow markers, transition animation, and default Graphviz colors.

Testing strategy:
- Run select_deselect, select_delete, draw_edge, rendering, and transition specs.

title: Verify overlay focus escape and scroll locking
description:
Summary:
Run focused checks for all migrated overlays and forms.

Acceptance criteria:
- Escape closes active overlays as before.
- Focus trap/restoration works for dialog, nested confirmation, menu, drawer, and snackbar cases.
- Body/page scroll locking does not break graph pan/zoom or editor scrolling.
- Cypress specs covering overlays pass or are updated for equivalent stable selectors.

Design notes:
- Behavior correctness is more important than visual polish in this task.

Edge cases:
- Nested confirmation from open-from-browser, dialog opened from menu, graph fullscreen plus overlay, and editor focus before opening settings.

Testing strategy:
- Run settings, browser_save_and_open, help, fullscreen_graph, pan_and_zoom, and smoke overlay specs.

title: Verify shell workflows against Cypress
description:
Summary:
Run and repair shell/navigation regressions introduced during shell migration.

Acceptance criteria:
- Relevant Cypress specs pass for toolbar, menus, fullscreen, GitHub link, settings entry, help entry, and graph pan/zoom entry points.
- Any selector updates are committed with the component changes that require them.
- No shell task remains accepted with known broken workflows.

Design notes:
- Test failures should be treated as behavior regressions unless proven to be stale Material-specific assertions.

Edge cases:
- Flaky graph rendering waits and portaled overlay timing.

Testing strategy:
- Run targeted Cypress specs first, then include shell smoke specs in full regression later.

title: Coordinate form and table primitive build
description:
## Summary
Coordinate the form/table primitive work. Do not implement this issue as one large slice; close it after its child tasks provide fields, option controls, table/sort controls, and event/accessibility validation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Child tasks cover input, number input, select, switch, checkbox, radio group, labels/helper text, table rows, sortable headers, and inline adornments.
- Inputs preserve current event contracts: event.target.value for text/numeric values and event.target.checked for boolean controls.
- Form controls expose accessible names and helper text relationships.

## Design Notes
- Forms should be compact and utilitarian, with visible grouping and direct labels.
- State should be apparent without relying on Material color conventions.

## Edge Cases
- Numeric settings stored as strings, select values from generated lists, long DOT snippets in tables, and disabled/hidden controls.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through settings, storage dialogs, format drawer, color picker, and open-from-browser table migrations.

title: Coordinate toolbar and button primitive build
description:
## Summary
Coordinate toolbar/button primitive work. Do not implement this issue as one broad slice; close it after child tasks provide button/link variants, toolbar grouping/layout, and accessibility/disabled-state verification.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Primitives cover current toolbar actions: menu, new, open, save, undo, redo, zoom controls, insert, node format, edge format, settings, GitHub, and help.
- Components preserve aria-labels, IDs, click handlers, disabled behavior, and keyboard focus behavior.
- Styling uses tokens and app CSS, not MUI or tss-react.

## Design Notes
- Buttons should feel like precise editor controls: compact, rectangular, high-contrast, and readable.
- Use familiar iconography for icon-only actions, with accessible labels.

## Edge Cases
- GitHub external link must remain a link with the same href.
- Toolbar overflow/narrow width must not hide core graph/editor actions without an alternate path.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through ButtonAppBar migration and toolbar Cypress specs.

title: Coordinate overlay primitive build
description:
## Summary
Coordinate the overlay primitive work. Do not implement this issue as one large slice; close it after its child tasks provide dialog, menu/popover/tooltip, drawer, toast/snackbar, and portal/focus validation coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Child tasks cover every overlay family needed to remove MUI: dialog, alert dialog, drawer, menu, popover, tooltip, toast/snackbar, close controls, portal layering, and focus behavior.
- Overlay primitives expose stable app-owned nodes for existing IDs and test selectors.
- Base UI provides behavior where appropriate; styling remains app-owned CSS.

## Design Notes
- Brutalist overlays should be plain, structural, and content-first: strong borders, no Material shadows, compact titles, direct actions.

## Edge Cases
- Graph fullscreen and dialogs must layer correctly.
- Escape should close the active overlay without corrupting editor focus state.
- Body scroll locking must not interfere with graph pan/zoom.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through the first migrated settings dialog, help menu, drawer, and updated snackbar.

title: Load custom font assets and global reset
description:
Summary:
Replace typeface-roboto with custom font loading and a reset that supports the brutalist/minimal direction.

Acceptance criteria:
- Roboto import is removed from src/pages/index.js once replacement typography is in place.
- Global CSS defines app font families, baseline line-height, focus outline, button/input inheritance, and portal stacking context.
- Font loading strategy is documented and does not block the editor from rendering.

Design notes:
- Use a distinctive display/control font and a legible editor-adjacent body font; avoid Roboto, Arial, Inter, Open Sans, and default system fallback as the primary identity.
- Typography must keep dense toolbars and dialogs readable.

Edge cases:
- Ace editor font size settings must continue to control editor content.
- Graph SVG labels are generated by Graphviz and should not be inadvertently restyled unless explicitly scoped.

Testing strategy:
- Run Cypress text editor font-size tests.
- Manually verify default, settings dialog, and narrow toolbar views.

title: Define brutalist minimal design tokens
description:
Summary:
Create app-owned CSS variables for spacing, borders, color, typography, z-index, and motion.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tokens cover app shell, toolbar, panes, overlays, controls, tables, focus rings, disabled states, graph chrome, and editor chrome.
- Token names describe intent rather than source library components.
- Tokens avoid Material UI shadows/elevation and one-note palettes.

Design notes:
- Use strong boundaries, compact rhythm, flat planes, explicit state changes, and restrained accent colors.
- Keep graph content visually primary; UI chrome should frame the work without competing with it.

Edge cases:
- Tokens must account for fullscreen mode and updated snackbar offset.
- Motion tokens must respect reduced-motion preferences.

Testing strategy:
- Verify tokens are consumed by at least one primitive and one shell surface before broad rollout.

title: Build custom brutalist UI foundation
description:
## Summary
Replace the Material UI theme/styling foundation with a custom, app-owned design system built on Base UI primitives, CSS, and custom fonts.

## Success Criteria
- The foundation provides tokens, global CSS, font loading, layout primitives, interaction states, and Base UI wrappers for the component families used by the app.
- New components are unstyled-by-default at the library layer and styled through app-owned CSS.
- Material UI and tss-react are not used for any new UI work after this foundation lands.

## Design Notes
- Direction: brutalist/minimal editor for graph work, with high contrast, strict grid, compact controls, obvious borders, direct labels, and custom typography.
- Avoid Material Design shadows, rounded cards, Roboto, generic gradients, and decorative chrome.
- Use Base UI because it is headless/unstyled and leaves CSS control in the app.

## Parallelization
- After design tokens exist, toolbar/button, overlay, form/table, disclosure, and icon work can proceed in parallel.
- Overlay and form/table work now have child tasks so different workers can own disjoint primitive families.
- Primitive usage documentation waits on the actual primitive families so it reflects implementation reality.

## Edge Cases
- Portals must layer correctly above the graph canvas and editor.
- CSS reset must not break Ace editor internals, d3-graphviz SVG output, or react-color widgets.
- Custom fonts must not change Ace editor font-size settings or Graphviz-generated SVG labels unexpectedly.

## Testing Strategy
- Build primitives with small integration examples in migrated components rather than isolated unused code.
- Run Jest/build after global CSS and dependency changes.

title: Install Base UI dependency behind foundation branch
description:
Summary:
Add the current Base UI React package and verify it works with React 18 and Create React App in this repository.

Acceptance criteria:
- package.json and lockfile include the selected Base UI package.
- Installation uses the current official package name @base-ui/react unless implementation research proves a different package is required.
- A minimal import from one Base UI primitive compiles in CRA.

Design notes:
- Base UI should provide accessible behavior while all styling stays in app-owned CSS.
- Do not introduce Tailwind or another styling framework for this migration.

Edge cases:
- Watch for ESM/CJS compatibility with react-scripts 5.
- Avoid adding duplicate headless libraries for the same primitive family.

Testing strategy:
- Run npm install, npm test -- --watchAll=false or targeted Jest, and npm run build after dependency changes.

title: Harden stable selector contract
description:
Summary:
Confirm the selector and accessibility contract that redesigned components must preserve.

Acceptance criteria:
- Existing IDs used by Cypress commands remain listed as required compatibility selectors.
- Any replacement selector is mapped one-for-one and updated in cypress/support/commands.js in the same task that changes the DOM.
- Interactive controls retain accessible names at least as specific as the current aria-labels and visible labels.

Design notes:
- Base UI internals may change wrapper markup; app-owned IDs should live on stable elements that reflect user actions.
- Avoid coupling tests to generated component structure.

Edge cases:
- Portaled dialogs and menus must still be discoverable by tests.
- Controls nested inside Base UI parts must expose stable click targets.

Testing strategy:
- Run Cypress specs that exercise toolbar, main menu, settings, and dialogs after selector changes.

title: Define no-functionality-change checklist
description:
Summary:
Create the working checklist each migration slice must satisfy before it is considered done.

Acceptance criteria:
- Checklist states that business logic, graph parsing, graph mutation, persistence keys, backend RPC/SSE contracts, and Cypress workflow outcomes must remain unchanged.
- Checklist requires before/after verification for each migrated component.
- Checklist defines when a visual-only change is allowed and when behavior review is required.

Design notes:
- The checklist should be short enough to use during every 5-30 minute task.
- The brutalist/minimal design direction must be constrained by the behavior contract.

Edge cases:
- Explicitly cover keyboard escape handling, focus restoration, and fullscreen exit behavior.

Testing strategy:
- Validate the checklist against at least one shell component and one dialog component before broad migration.

title: Audit current MUI touchpoints and behavior owners
description:
Summary:
Build a migration inventory for every file importing @mui/material, @mui/icons-material, tss-react/mui, typeface-roboto, or theme helpers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Inventory lists the source file, MUI components/hooks used, current DOM IDs/classes relied on by Cypress, and owning user workflow.
- Inventory identifies files with high behavior risk: src/pages/index.js, Graph.js, TextEditor.js, SettingsDialog.js, FormatDrawer.js, OpenFromBrowserDialog.js, and ButtonAppBar.js.
- Inventory separates purely visual wrappers from components that manage focus, portals, selection, persistence, or keyboard behavior.

Design notes:
- Use this audit to plan adapter boundaries, not to redesign functionality.
- Keep the app's graph/editor model untouched.

Edge cases:
- Include indirect MUI dependence through withRoot and SvgIcon.
- Include visual state currently encoded as MUI elevation, transitions, or theme spacing.

Testing strategy:
- Cross-check inventory against rg results for @mui, tss-react, typeface-roboto, useTheme, withStyles, and CssBaseline.

title: Coordinate current interaction baseline capture
description:
## Summary
Coordinate current-state baseline capture. Do not implement this issue as one broad pass; close it after its child tasks capture graph/editor, overlay/storage/settings, and responsive/backend/update-notification baselines.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Child tasks cover every protected workflow needed before component migration.
- Baseline notes distinguish automated test coverage from manual verification steps.
- Any known flaky or hard-to-observe behavior is called out with reproduction steps.

## Design Notes
- Baseline is functional, not aesthetic. Screenshots can support layout comparison but must not become approval for changing workflows.

## Edge Cases
- Include persisted settings and projects from localStorage.
- Include graph transition settings because animations can hide behavior regressions.

## Testing Strategy
- Run representative Cypress specs and capture pass/fail state through child tasks.

title: Preserve existing editor behavior during UI rewrite
description:
## Summary
Create a behavior-first safety net before replacing Material UI. The redesign must not change DOT editing, graph rendering, visual editing, persistence, export/import, settings, keyboard shortcuts, backend sync, update notifications, or existing Cypress-facing selectors.

## Success Criteria
- Every migration task can point to a baseline behavior source: current code, existing Cypress spec, new smoke check, or explicit manual checklist item.
- Existing public IDs, localStorage keys, backend contracts, and test helpers remain stable unless a task explicitly updates tests for semantically equivalent markup.
- No visual redesign work lands without a way to prove graph/editor behavior stayed intact.

## Design Notes
- Treat the app as an expert tool for editing Graphviz/DOT, not a marketing surface.
- Preserve dense, command-oriented workflows while changing only presentation and component internals.
- Brutalist/minimal direction should increase clarity through typography, grid, contrast, and restraint rather than adding new flows.

## Parallelization
- The MUI touchpoint audit, baseline capture, selector contract, and no-functionality checklist can run in parallel after this epic is claimed.
- Smoke coverage waits for the workflow coverage map so it protects the right states.
- Foundation work can begin after the checklist, but component migration waits for smoke coverage.

## Edge Cases
- LocalStorage restoration, URL import, export dialogs, backend SSE sync, update snackbar visibility, focus handoff between panes, and fullscreen graph mode are all part of the protected behavior surface.

## Testing Strategy
- Run existing Jest/Cypress coverage before component swaps.
- Add targeted smoke coverage for view states that currently lack stable regression checks.
- Compare behavior before and after each migration slice rather than waiting for the final removal.

title: Migrate graph loading and fullscreen controls
description:
## Summary
Replace Graph.js MUI Fade, CircularProgress, IconButton, and fullscreen icons with custom primitives.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Loading state and fullscreen button IDs/labels remain stable.
- Fullscreen open/close handlers and layout behavior are unchanged.
- No MUI imports remain for graph chrome controls.

## Design Notes
- Loading and fullscreen chrome should be minimal and avoid covering graph content.

## Edge Cases
- Slow render, fullscreen from small viewport, and transition during render.

## Testing Strategy
- Run fullscreen_graph and rendering specs.

title: Preserve graph zoom and mouse registration
description:
## Summary
Verify Graph.js ref/event behavior survives visual chrome migration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- registerZoomInButtonClick, registerZoomOutButtonClick, registerZoomOutMapButtonClick, and registerZoomResetButtonClick still expose working handlers.
- Pan, zoom, drag selection, node selection, edge selection, and edge drawing behavior remain unchanged.
- Graph DOM IDs/classes used by Cypress remain stable.

## Design Notes
- Do not refactor graph logic while migrating visual chrome.

## Edge Cases
- Ctrl/shift selection, click outside graph, drag from insert panel, and graph transition timing.

## Testing Strategy
- Run pan_and_zoom, draw_edge, select_deselect, and select_delete specs.

title: Migrate format style controls
description:
## Summary
Migrate style switch and style checkbox controls in FormatDrawer.

## Acceptance Criteria
- style-switch, styles group, and per-style checkbox IDs remain stable.
- onStyleChange receives the same style payloads as before.
- Hidden/disabled style states match current behavior.

## Design Notes
- Style options should be compact but easy to distinguish as toggles.

## Edge Cases
- Multiple styles, no default style, rapid checkbox changes, and node vs edge available styles.

## Testing Strategy
- Manual node/edge style checks and relevant insert/select specs.

title: Migrate format color controls
description:
## Summary
Migrate color and fillcolor switches plus ColorPicker integration in FormatDrawer.

## Acceptance Criteria
- color-switch, fillcolor-switch, color-picker, and fillcolor-picker IDs remain stable.
- onColorChange and onFillColorChange receive the same values as before.
- Hidden/visible color picker behavior is unchanged.

## Design Notes
- Pair visible swatches with compact input controls.

## Edge Cases
- Empty/default colors, invalid color text, rapid picker changes, and edge fillcolor behavior.

## Testing Strategy
- Manual node/edge color checks plus color picker task verification.

title: Preserve saved-graph delete confirmation flow
description:
## Summary
Verify OpenFromBrowserDialog delete action still opens and resolves the delete confirmation flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Delete button remains accessible and testable.
- Confirmation dialog layers over the open dialog and returns focus predictably.
- Project deletion updates the table and selection state exactly as before.

## Design Notes
- Destructive action should be visually explicit without adding steps.

## Edge Cases
- Delete selected graph, delete unselected graph, delete final graph, and cancel confirmation.

## Testing Strategy
- Run browser_save_and_open delete checks.

title: Migrate format drawer shell
description:
## Summary
Replace FormatDrawer's MUI Drawer, title, close icon, and divider shell.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Drawer root ID, close button ID, side placement, width, and close handler remain stable.
- Node and edge drawer variants preserve title and layout placement.
- No MUI Drawer/DialogTitle/Divider/IconButton usage remains for the shell.

## Design Notes
- The drawer should read as a compact inspector panel.

## Edge Cases
- Left/right placement, fullscreen graph, and focus handoff from graph/editor.

## Testing Strategy
- Manual open/close checks for node and edge drawers plus related Cypress flows.

title: Migrate saved-graph dialog shell and table
description:
## Summary
Replace OpenFromBrowserDialog shell and saved-graph table markup with custom dialog/table primitives.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dialog root ID, title, description, close/cancel/open actions, table header IDs, and row selectors remain stable.
- Empty and populated project lists render correctly.
- No MUI Dialog/Table usage remains for the shell/table.

## Design Notes
- Use dense table rows with clear selected state.

## Edge Cases
- Empty project list, long graph names, long DOT snippets, and narrow dialog widths.

## Testing Strategy
- Run browser_save_and_open table presence checks.

title: Preserve saved-graph sorting selection and open behavior
description:
## Summary
Verify and preserve OpenFromBrowserDialog sorting, row selection, and open behavior after table migration.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Name/source/time sorting matches current stableSort/getSorting behavior.
- Selected row state and Open action use the same selected project name.
- Current Cypress table helpers still pass or are updated for equivalent stable selectors.

## Design Notes
- Sorting affordance should be obvious without Material TableSortLabel.

## Edge Cases
- Duplicate timestamps, duplicate DOT source, and opening after sort order changes.

## Testing Strategy
- Run browser_save_and_open and manual sort/open checks.

title: Migrate settings viewing and tween controls
description:
## Summary
Migrate fit graph, transition duration, path/shape tween switches, and tween precision controls.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Switches preserve event.target.checked semantics and IDs.
- Transition duration and tween precision inputs preserve string value behavior.
- Tween precision radio group updates adornment text between points and percent as before.

## Design Notes
- Group transition controls tightly so state relationships are easy to scan.

## Edge Cases
- Percent/point unit switching, decimal values, disabled tween combinations, and localStorage restoration.

## Testing Strategy
- Run transition spec and settings controls checks.

title: Migrate settings text-editor controls
description:
## Summary
Migrate font size, tab size, and holdoff controls in SettingsDialog.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Font size updates Ace editor chrome exactly as before.
- Tab size and holdoff preserve current numeric/string handler contracts.
- Helper text remains associated with each field.

## Design Notes
- Keep editor settings compact and aligned with other numeric fields.

## Edge Cases
- Backspace/type sequences used by Cypress, slow render holdoff, and reload persistence.

## Testing Strategy
- Run text_editor specs covering font size, tab size, and holdoff.

title: Migrate settings dialog shell and sections
description:
## Summary
Replace SettingsDialog's dialog shell, title/close control, content structure, and section dividers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dialog root ID, close behavior, escape behavior, and section order remain stable.
- Graph Rendering, Graph Viewing, and Text Editor sections remain recognizable and compact.
- No MUI Dialog/DialogTitle/DialogContent usage remains for the shell.

## Design Notes
- Use structural dividers and compact headings, not nested cards.

## Edge Cases
- Dialog opened from toolbar versus main menu and close by escape versus close button.

## Testing Strategy
- Run settings dialog open/close spec path.

title: Migrate settings rendering controls
description:
## Summary
Migrate the Graph rendering section controls in SettingsDialog.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Engine select preserves value, options, IDs, and onEngineSelectChange behavior.
- Form label/helper text relationships remain accessible.
- No MUI Select/Input/FormControl usage remains for this section.

## Design Notes
- Keep engine selection dense and direct.

## Edge Cases
- Generated engine list, current engine not first in list, and keyboard selection.

## Testing Strategy
- Run settings spec and any rendering-engine checks available.

title: Replace GitHub icon wrapper and toolbar link
description:
## Summary
Replace GitHubIcon.js usage of MUI SvgIcon and migrate the toolbar GitHub link to custom controls.

## Acceptance Criteria
- GitHub link href remains https://github.com/magjac/graphviz-visual-editor.
- Link remains accessible and testable through the current Cypress helper.
- No MUI SvgIcon import remains in GitHubIcon.js.

## Design Notes
- Use an app-owned SVG/icon wrapper aligned with the custom icon sizing system.

## Edge Cases
- External link target/rel attributes and icon-only accessible name.

## Testing Strategy
- Run github spec and toolbar smoke checks.

title: Migrate toolbar file and history actions
description:
## Summary
Migrate menu, new, open, save-as, undo, and redo toolbar controls in ButtonAppBar.js.

## Acceptance Criteria
- IDs, aria labels, disabled states, and handler calls match current behavior.
- Button group spacing and focus states use custom toolbar primitives.
- No MUI Button/IconButton usage remains for these controls.

## Design Notes
- Group file/history actions as compact editor commands.

## Edge Cases
- Disabled undo/redo and menu anchor behavior.

## Testing Strategy
- Run main_menu, browser_save_and_open entry paths, and undo_redo specs.

title: Migrate toolbar zoom format and settings actions
description:
## Summary
Migrate zoom in/out/fit/reset, Insert, Node, Edge, Settings, and Help controls in ButtonAppBar.js.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Existing handler registration and click behavior remains unchanged.
- IDs and aria labels remain stable for Cypress helpers.
- Text/icon controls fit without overlap at standard viewport widths.

## Design Notes
- Keep graph operations grouped separately from file/history actions.

## Edge Cases
- Graph not yet rendered, fullscreen transitions, and narrow toolbar widths.

## Testing Strategy
- Run pan_and_zoom, insert_node, settings, help, and smoke toolbar specs.

title: Build table and sortable-header primitives
description:
## Summary
Build table primitives for saved graph lists and help/reference tables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Supports dense rows, header cells, sortable header affordance, row selection state, action cells, and stable IDs/selectors.
- Long DOT source snippets and names truncate/wrap predictably without layout overflow.
- Sorting affordance remains keyboard and screen-reader understandable.

## Design Notes
- Tables should feel like utilitarian data grids, not card lists.

## Edge Cases
- Empty state, duplicate names, long DOT snippets, and narrow dialogs.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through OpenFromBrowserDialog and help dialog migrations.

title: Build toast and snackbar primitive
description:
## Summary
Build the app-owned toast/snackbar primitive needed to replace UpdatedSnackbar.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Supports controlled open/close, close affordance, stable testable root, and layout offset coordination with the app shell.
- Does not trap focus or interfere with graph/editor keyboard input.
- Uses brutalist/minimal notification styling without Material elevation.

## Design Notes
- Treat app notices as compact system bars or framed notices rather than floating cards.

## Edge Cases
- First load after version change, repeated close, fullscreen graph, and narrow viewport overlap.

## Testing Strategy
- Validate through UpdatedSnackbar migration and shell smoke checks.

title: Migrate updated snackbar toast
description:
## Summary
Replace MUI Snackbar, SnackbarContent, IconButton, CloseIcon, and tss styling in UpdatedSnackbar.js with the custom notification primitive.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The update notification opens based on the existing version/localStorage logic and closes through the same handler.
- The notification keeps its current interaction contract with src/pages/index.js, including layout offset while visible.
- Close control remains accessible and testable, with no MUI/tss imports left in UpdatedSnackbar.js.

## Design Notes
- Treat the snackbar as a compact system notice, not a floating Material card. Use strong border, direct copy, and clear close affordance.

## Edge Cases
- First load after version change, repeated close/open across reloads, fullscreen graph state, and narrow viewport overlap.

## Testing Strategy
- Run shell smoke checks and any version/update snackbar manual check needed to confirm localStorage behavior.

title: Audit accessibility and keyboard behavior
description:
Summary:
Perform a final accessibility and keyboard audit of the redesigned interface.

Acceptance criteria:
- Toolbar, menus, dialogs, drawers, settings controls, tables, and graph fullscreen controls are keyboard reachable.
- Focus indicators are visible and consistent with the custom design system.
- Dialog labels/descriptions, form labels/helper text, and icon-only button labels are present.
- Reduced motion preference is respected for app-authored motion.

Design notes:
- Brutalist/minimal styling should make focus and state more explicit, not less.

Edge cases:
- Portaled overlays, nested confirmations, disabled controls, and hidden color pickers.

Testing strategy:
- Manual keyboard pass plus targeted Cypress assertions for focus/escape where practical.

title: Re-skin color picker shell
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI FormControl/Input usage in ColorPicker.js while preserving react-color behavior and payloads.

Acceptance criteria:
- Color text input and picker still update color values exactly as before.
- IDs and event handlers used by FormatDrawer remain stable.
- Color picker visibility controlled by drawer switches is unchanged.

Design notes:
- Use a simple swatch/input pairing that fits the inspector drawer.

Edge cases:
- Empty color, invalid color text, rapid picker changes, and separate color/fillcolor instances.

Testing strategy:
- Manually verify node and edge color/fillcolor changes and run format-related specs.

title: Coordinate graph viewport chrome migration
description:
## Summary
Coordinate the Graph.js visual chrome migration. Do not implement this issue as one large slice; close it after child tasks migrate loading/fullscreen controls, preserve zoom/mouse registration, protect selection/SVG styling, and run graph regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Canvas rendering, loading state, fullscreen open/close, zoom registration, pan/zoom, selection, deletion, drag selection, and edge drawing behavior are unchanged.
- Graph control IDs remain stable, including fullscreen.
- Loading state is accessible and visually aligned with the custom system.

## Design Notes
- Graph chrome should be minimal and should not obscure SVG content.

## Edge Cases
- Slow graph render, transition animation, fullscreen mode, mouse drag interactions, and selected component styling.

## Testing Strategy
- Run rendering, fullscreen_graph, pan_and_zoom, select_deselect, select_delete, draw_edge, and transition specs after child tasks land.

title: Redesign graph and editor work surfaces
description:
## Summary
Apply the new visual language to the graph canvas, text editor chrome, preview surfaces, color picker shell, and graph controls without touching graph parsing or mutation behavior.

## Success Criteria
- Graph rendering, selection, pan/zoom, visual editing, DOT editing, error display, previews, color picking, and fullscreen behavior match current workflows.
- MUI/tss-react imports are removed from Graph.js, TextEditor.js, DotSrcPreview.js, SvgPreview.js, ColorPicker.js, and GitHubIcon.js where applicable.
- Third-party internals for Ace, d3-graphviz, and react-color remain stable.

## Design Notes
- The graph and DOT editor are the product. UI chrome should make them feel precise and controlled.

## Parallelization
- Text editor chrome, graph chrome, previews, and color picker shell can run in parallel after the smoke baseline and required primitives are complete.
- Graph chrome has nested subtasks because it touches fullscreen/loading controls and d3 event registration.
- SVG styling preservation and end-to-end graph/editor verification wait on the relevant work-surface migrations.

## Edge Cases
- Generated SVG content must not be globally restyled by the new CSS system.
- Ace editor and react-color internals should be scoped away from the global reset.

## Testing Strategy
- Run rendering, text_editor, pan_and_zoom, select/deselect, select_delete, draw_edge, transition, fullscreen_graph, and color/format checks.

title: Re-skin text editor chrome and error affordance
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI IconButton/ErrorOutline and tss styles in TextEditor.js with app primitives while leaving Ace editor behavior untouched.

Acceptance criteria:
- Text editor wrapper, error button, tooltip trigger, font-size setting, tab-size setting, and parser error highlighting behave as before.
- Existing IDs including text-editor-wrapper, text-editor, and error-button remain stable.
- Ace internal CSS is not overwritten unexpectedly.

Design notes:
- Editor chrome should be restrained and dense, with an unmistakable error affordance.

Edge cases:
- Error tooltip positioning, long syntax errors, very large DOT sources, and font size changes.

Testing strategy:
- Run text_editor spec, especially parser error and font/tab-size cases.

title: Migrate insert panels accordion
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Accordion, Typography, and expand icon usage in InsertPanels.js.

Acceptance criteria:
- Shape categories expand/collapse and shape insertion/drag behavior is unchanged.
- Existing category and shape presentation remains functional with the new aesthetic.
- The panel remains usable alongside graph/editor panes.

Design notes:
- Use sharp collapsible sections and compact shape tiles; avoid Material accordion styling.

Edge cases:
- Large shape lists, drag from panel to canvas, and narrow pane widths.

Testing strategy:
- Run insert_node spec and manual drag/drop shape insertion.

title: Coordinate format drawer migration
description:
## Summary
Coordinate the FormatDrawer migration. Do not implement this issue as one large slice; close it after child tasks migrate the drawer shell, style controls, color/fillcolor controls, and node/edge formatting checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Node and edge drawers open/close and call onStyleChange, onColorChange, and onFillColorChange with the same payloads.
- Style switches, style checkboxes, color switch, fillcolor switch, and color pickers preserve IDs and behavior.
- Drawer width/position and graph/editor layout interactions remain equivalent.

## Design Notes
- Drawer should feel like a compact inspector panel with clear control grouping and visible state.

## Edge Cases
- Default attributes absent, multiple style values, color picker hidden/visible transitions, and node vs edge type differences.

## Testing Strategy
- Run insert_node, select/delete, and manual node/edge formatting checks after child tasks land.

title: Migrate export SVG dialog
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI usage in ExportAsSvgDialog.js while preserving SVG filename entry and export behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Filename input, cancel, close, and export SVG actions behave as before.
- Existing IDs remain stable.
- Dialog uses app dialog/input/button primitives.

Design notes:
- Keep file action hierarchy clear and compact.

Edge cases:
- Empty filenames, repeated export attempts, and close without export.

Testing strategy:
- Run export SVG coverage and manual browser download behavior if Cypress cannot assert download reliably.

title: Migrate destructive confirmation dialogs
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI usage in DoYouWantToDeleteDialog.js and DoYouWantToReplaceItDialog.js.

Acceptance criteria:
- Cancel/delete and cancel/replace handlers fire exactly as before.
- Dialog IDs, action IDs, and close behavior remain stable.
- Destructive action is visually distinct without changing copy or flow unnecessarily.

Design notes:
- Use the same alert dialog primitive for both confirmations.

Edge cases:
- Nested confirmation launched from an already open dialog must layer and restore focus correctly.

Testing strategy:
- Run browser save/open delete and replace-flow specs.

title: Coordinate open-from-browser dialog migration
description:
## Summary
Coordinate the OpenFromBrowserDialog migration. Do not implement this issue as one large slice; close it after child tasks migrate the dialog shell/table, sorting/selection/open behavior, delete confirmation flow, and regression checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Saved graph list renders name, DOT source, timestamp, selection, sorting, opening, and deletion exactly as before.
- Delete action opens the confirmation dialog and preserves selected graph behavior.
- Table headers and row IDs/selectors used by Cypress remain stable.

## Design Notes
- Use a high-density table with visible row states and brutalist header affordances.

## Edge Cases
- Empty project list, duplicate DOT source, long graph names, long DOT source snippets, and delete while a row is selected.

## Testing Strategy
- Run browser_save_and_open and main menu open-flow specs after child tasks land.

title: Migrate save and rename browser dialog flow
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI usage in SaveAsToBrowserDialog.js and preserve the replace-existing graph flow.

Acceptance criteria:
- Name input, cancel, save, close, duplicate-name detection, and replace confirmation behavior are unchanged.
- Dialog IDs and action IDs used by Cypress remain stable.
- The dialog uses app dialog and input primitives.

Design notes:
- Keep copy direct and action hierarchy clear.

Edge cases:
- Empty name, existing name, rename flow, and repeated open/close without losing typed value unexpectedly.

Testing strategy:
- Run browser_save_and_open and main_menu rename/save specs.

title: Migrate export URL dialog
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI usage in ExportAsUrlDialog.js while preserving generated URL, copy, open-link, and cancel behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Exported URL content and actions behave exactly as before.
- Copy/open/cancel IDs remain stable.
- Dialog focus and close behavior match the existing workflow.

Design notes:
- URL presentation should be selectable, compact, and clearly separated from actions.

Edge cases:
- Very long DOT sources produce long URLs; ensure layout does not overflow unusably.

Testing strategy:
- Run export_and_import_as_url spec.

title: Migrate dialogs drawers and forms
description:
## Summary
Replace Material UI across settings, storage/export dialogs, confirmation dialogs, help/about overlays, format drawers, insert panels, tables, and form controls.

## Success Criteria
- Dialog, drawer, table, input, select, switch, checkbox, radio, disclosure/collapsible, and action button usage is migrated to Base UI/custom primitives.
- All dialog/drawer IDs, values, callbacks, escape handling, and persistence behavior remain intact.
- Accessibility improves or remains equivalent for labels, focus traps, descriptions, and keyboard navigation.

## Design Notes
- Brutalist/minimal overlays should prioritize hierarchy and speed: dense sections, visible dividers, direct action rows, no card nesting.

## Parallelization
- Settings, open-from-browser, save/rename, export URL, export SVG, confirmations, help/about, format drawer, and insert panels can run in parallel after their primitive dependencies and smoke baseline are complete.
- Settings, open-from-browser, and format drawer have nested subtasks because they are larger workflows.
- Final overlay/focus verification waits on every migrated overlay, drawer, form, and disclosure slice.

## Edge Cases
- Many settings values are persisted as strings; do not normalize types unless existing handlers already do so.
- Nested confirmations and portaled dialogs must restore focus correctly.

## Testing Strategy
- Run settings, transition, browser save/open, export/import, help, text editor, insert node, and format drawer related specs.

title: Coordinate settings dialog migration
description:
## Summary
Coordinate the SettingsDialog migration. Do not implement this issue as one large slice; close it after its child tasks migrate the shell/sections, graph rendering controls, graph viewing/transition controls, text editor controls, and targeted regression coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Engine select, fit graph switch, transition duration, path/shape tween switches, tween precision radio/input/adornment, font size, tab size, and holdoff controls keep current values and handlers.
- Dialog opens/closes by toolbar, menu, close button, and escape as before.
- IDs used by Cypress remain stable.

## Design Notes
- Use compact grouped sections with strong labels and dividers.
- Preserve the existing Graph rendering, Graph viewing, and Text Editor grouping.

## Edge Cases
- Numeric controls currently pass event.target.value strings.
- Tween precision adornment changes between points and percent.

## Testing Strategy
- Run settings, transition, and text_editor specs that modify settings after child tasks land.

title: Replace shell Paper and Grid layout
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Paper/Grid usage in src/pages/index.js with CSS grid/flex layout that preserves current panes and sizing.

Acceptance criteria:
- Text editor, insert panels, format drawers, and graph canvas occupy the same functional regions as before.
- Existing height calculations for toolbar, padding, snackbar, and fullscreen are preserved or simplified without behavioral change.
- MUI Paper elevation is replaced by tokenized focus/active styling.

Design notes:
- Use visible pane boundaries and a utilitarian layout grid rather than cards or shadows.

Edge cases:
- Fullscreen should remove shell chrome from the graph area as before.
- Updated snackbar open state should not obscure controls or graph content.

Testing strategy:
- Run fullscreen_graph, rendering, text_editor, insert_node, and visual smoke specs.

title: Preserve pane focus and open-state behavior
description:
Summary:
Recreate the current focus and open-state visual behavior without relying on MUI elevation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Text editor, node format drawer, edge format drawer, insert panels, and graph pane still reflect focus/open state.
- setFocus and setFocusIf flows in src/pages/index.js remain functionally unchanged.
- Visual styling communicates focus without shadows/elevation.

Design notes:
- Use border weight, background plane, and high-contrast focus rings instead of elevation.

Edge cases:
- Clicking outside the document and returning should preserve the existing blur/focus semantics.

Testing strategy:
- Manually verify pane focus transitions and run Cypress specs that click editor, graph, insert, and drawers.

title: Coordinate main toolbar rebuild
description:
## Summary
Coordinate the ButtonAppBar migration. Do not implement this issue as one large slice; close it after its child tasks migrate action groups, title/status area, GitHub icon/link, responsive behavior, and toolbar regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All current toolbar controls render with the same IDs, aria labels, disabled states, and handler calls.
- Insert, Node, Edge, zoom, settings, GitHub, and help actions remain visible and usable.
- No MUI/tss/icon imports remain in ButtonAppBar.js or GitHubIcon.js after child tasks complete.

## Design Notes
- Use grouped rectangular controls with clear active/disabled states and no Material elevation.
- Preserve density while making clickable targets reliable.

## Edge Cases
- Empty graph name, long graph name, narrow viewport, disabled undo/redo, and external GitHub link.

## Testing Strategy
- Run main_menu, github, undo_redo, pan_and_zoom, insert_node, and smoke toolbar specs after child tasks land.

title: Rebuild main and help menus using Base UI
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Menu/MenuItem in MainMenu.js and HelpMenu.js with Base UI menu primitives and app styling.

Acceptance criteria:
- Menu items keep current IDs and call the same handlers.
- Menu open/close behavior, anchor positioning, keyboard navigation, escape, and outside click behavior are preserved.
- Main menu and help menu use the same app menu primitive.

Design notes:
- Menus should feel like command lists: compact rows, sharp separators, direct labels.

Edge cases:
- Menu opened from toolbar should restore focus predictably after selection or escape.
- Portaled menu must not appear behind graph SVG or toolbar.

Testing strategy:
- Run main_menu, help, settings, export/import, and keyboard navigation smoke checks.

title: Migrate app shell and navigation
description:
## Summary
Replace Material UI in the root shell, toolbar, menus, update snackbar, and high-level layout while preserving app state and workflow behavior.

## Success Criteria
- src/pages/index.js, withRoot.js, ButtonAppBar.js, MainMenu.js, HelpMenu.js, UpdatedSnackbar.js, and root layout styling no longer depend on MUI/tss-react after this epic.
- Toolbar/menu/snackbar actions call the same handlers with the same data.
- Pane focus/open/fullscreen/update-snackbar layout behavior is unchanged.

## Design Notes
- The shell should read as a dense graph editing instrument: strict grid, compact toolbar groups, visible pane boundaries, and minimal ornament.

## Parallelization
- Toolbar, menus, snackbar, and root layout can proceed in parallel once their primitive dependencies and smoke baseline are complete.
- Pane focus and responsive work should wait until the shell layout and toolbar controls exist.
- Shell workflow verification waits on every shell slice, including the snackbar migration.

## Edge Cases
- Updated snackbar offset, fullscreen graph mode, long graph names, narrow toolbar width, and focus elevation replacement are high-risk layout states.

## Testing Strategy
- Run main_menu, github, fullscreen_graph, pan_and_zoom, settings open path, help, update snackbar checks, and smoke layout specs.

title: Replace root theme wrapper with CSS infrastructure
description:
Summary:
Remove the MUI ThemeProvider/CssBaseline path and replace it with app-owned global CSS and root providers needed by Base UI.

Acceptance criteria:
- withRoot.js is removed or reduced to non-MUI concerns.
- Root app layout applies global reset, font variables, color tokens, and portal isolation.
- No behavior in src/pages/index.js changes as part of this task.

Design notes:
- Keep provider surface minimal so component behavior stays visible in the component itself.

Edge cases:
- Ensure portaled overlays appear above graph and editor panes.
- Do not break CRA mounting in src/index.js.

Testing strategy:
- Run npm test target that imports the app and npm run build.

title: Document primitive usage conventions
description:
Summary:
Document how migrated components should use the new primitives and CSS tokens.

Acceptance criteria:
- Notes cover when to use each primitive, how to preserve IDs/aria labels, how to handle portals, and how to avoid behavior changes.
- Notes include examples for a toolbar button, dialog action row, form field, and table row.
- Notes explicitly ban new MUI/tss-react usage.

Design notes:
- Keep guidance implementation-focused and short enough for migration tasks.

Edge cases:
- Include how to handle third-party surfaces like Ace, react-color, and d3-generated SVG.

Testing strategy:
- Use the conventions while migrating the first shell component and revise if they are incomplete.

title: Replace Material icon dependency
description:
Summary:
Choose and apply a non-MUI icon strategy for toolbar, dialog, drawer, and graph controls.

Acceptance criteria:
- All @mui/icons-material imports have an app-owned or alternate-library replacement plan.
- Icons preserve recognizable semantics for new/open/save/undo/redo/zoom/settings/help/close/delete/fullscreen/chevrons.
- Icon sizing aligns with custom toolbar/button primitives.

Design notes:
- Prefer crisp geometric icons that match the brutalist/minimal tone.
- Avoid decorative icons where text is clearer.

Edge cases:
- GitHubIcon currently extends MUI SvgIcon; replace it without changing the GitHub link workflow.

Testing strategy:
- Run Cypress specs that click icon-only controls by ID or aria-label.

title: Add visual smoke coverage for core view states
description:
Summary:
Add lightweight smoke checks for major view states that are likely to break during a ground-up visual rewrite.

Acceptance criteria:
- Smoke coverage opens initial editor, graph fullscreen, settings dialog, open-from-browser dialog with saved data, format drawer, insert panels, and help/about overlays.
- Checks assert presence, sizing sanity, and absence of obvious overlap rather than pixel-perfect snapshots.
- Tests are resilient to the planned aesthetic change.

Design notes:
- Coverage should protect layout integrity without freezing Material UI visuals.

Edge cases:
- Include at least one narrow viewport because toolbar density and portaled overlays are likely to regress there.

Testing strategy:
- Add Cypress smoke checks or extend existing specs with minimal assertions.
- Run the new checks before and after one migrated component to prove they are useful.

title: Verify help menu dialog flows
description:
## Summary
Verify HelpMenu entries and migrated help/about dialogs as one workflow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Keyboard shortcuts, mouse operations, and about entries open the correct dialogs.
- Escape, close button, and focus restoration work consistently for all three dialogs.
- HelpMenu closes after selection and does not leave stale anchors or overlays.

## Design Notes
- This is workflow verification, not visual polish.

## Edge Cases
- Opening dialogs repeatedly, switching entries, and closing via escape from each dialog.

## Testing Strategy
- Run help spec and focused manual keyboard pass.

title: Migrate mouse operations dialog
description:
## Summary
Replace MUI usage in MouseOperationsDialog.js.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dialog opens from HelpMenu and closes by close button/escape as before.
- Mouse operation table content remains unchanged.
- IDs and accessible labels remain stable or are updated with equivalent selectors.

## Design Notes
- Present operations as a compact reference table.

## Edge Cases
- Long operation descriptions and narrow dialog width.

## Testing Strategy
- Run help spec and manual mouse operations dialog check.

title: Migrate about dialog
description:
## Summary
Replace MUI usage in AboutDialog.js.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dialog opens from HelpMenu and closes by close button/escape as before.
- Graphviz version, app version, repository/reference links, and existing content remain accurate and unchanged unless already generated by runtime values.
- No MUI/tss imports remain in AboutDialog.js.

## Design Notes
- Keep about content factual and compact.

## Edge Cases
- Long version strings and external links.

## Testing Strategy
- Run help/about coverage and manual link checks if Cypress does not cover them.

title: Migrate keyboard shortcuts dialog
description:
## Summary
Replace MUI usage in KeyboardShortcutsDialog.js.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dialog opens from HelpMenu and closes by close button/escape as before.
- Shortcut table/list content remains unchanged.
- IDs and accessible labels remain stable or are updated with equivalent selectors.

## Design Notes
- Use dense readable rows with strong dividers.

## Edge Cases
- Long shortcut labels and narrow dialog width.

## Testing Strategy
- Run help spec and manual keyboard dialog check.

title: Verify toolbar responsive behavior
description:
## Summary
Verify the rebuilt toolbar across desktop and narrow viewports.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Controls do not overlap, resize unpredictably, or push graph/editor panes offscreen.
- Critical actions remain reachable at narrow widths.
- Long graph names are contained without hiding command groups.

## Design Notes
- Prefer compact grouping and predictable wrapping over hiding core actions.

## Edge Cases
- Browser zoom, long graph names, disabled controls, and narrow viewport heights.

## Testing Strategy
- Run shell smoke checks at desktop and narrow viewport sizes.

title: Document migration notes and release risk
description:
Summary:
Write concise handoff notes after the redesign migration is complete.

Acceptance criteria:
- Notes summarize migrated component families, removed dependencies, new primitives, custom fonts, known residual risks, and exact test commands/results.
- Notes list any intentionally changed selectors or accessible names.
- Notes identify any deferred polish as new Beads issues rather than inline TODOs.

Design notes:
- Keep documentation operational and concrete.

Edge cases:
- If full Cypress could not run locally, document the blocker and targeted coverage that did run.

Testing strategy:
- Confirm notes match git diff, package changes, and test output before final handoff.

title: Re-skin DOT and SVG preview components
description:
Summary:
Replace MUI Card/CardContent/useTheme/tss usage in DotSrcPreview.js and SvgPreview.js.

Acceptance criteria:
- DOT source previews and SVG thumbnails render with the same content and hover interactions.
- Thumbnail event listeners still attach and detach correctly.
- Preview styling uses app CSS tokens and does not rely on MUI theme values.

Design notes:
- Previews should look like flat inspection panes, not Material cards.

Edge cases:
- Missing SVG, malformed SVG, hover enter/leave, and long DOT source.

Testing strategy:
- Run insert panel/preview-related smoke checks and manual hover preview verification.

title: Coordinate help and about dialog migration
description:
## Summary
Coordinate help/reference dialog migration. Do not implement this issue as one broad slice; close it after child tasks migrate keyboard shortcuts, mouse operations, about dialog, and help-menu verification.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Help menu entries open the correct dialogs.
- Tables/lists preserve current content and close behavior.
- About dialog still displays graphviz/app version details and GitHub/reference links as before.

## Design Notes
- Present reference material in dense readable tables with strong headings and no decorative framing.

## Edge Cases
- Long shortcut labels, mouse operation descriptions, and version string changes.

## Testing Strategy
- Run help and smoke dialog specs after child tasks complete.

title: Adapt responsive toolbar and panes
description:
Summary:
Ensure the redesigned shell remains usable on narrow and standard desktop viewports without hiding critical functionality.

Acceptance criteria:
- Toolbar controls wrap, group, or compact predictably without overlapping text or graph/editor panes.
- Dialog triggers and graph controls remain reachable at narrow widths.
- No critical editor workflow is removed on mobile/narrow screens.

Design notes:
- Use compact icon buttons and grouped controls; avoid a marketing-style responsive layout.

Edge cases:
- Long graph names, browser zoom, and small viewport heights.

Testing strategy:
- Run Cypress smoke checks at desktop and narrow viewport sizes.
